title: LROC color-shaded Moon texture, equirectangular 2K
source: NASA Goddard Scientific Visualization Studio (CGI Moon Kit, SVS 4720)
author: Ernie Wright, NASA Goddard
attributionText: Moon texture: NASA Goddard / Ernie Wright, CGI Moon Kit (SVS 4720).
alt: The Moon's near side in natural color, from the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Camera.

title: LROC color-shaded Moon texture, desktop high-DPI
source: NASA Goddard Scientific Visualization Studio (CGI Moon Kit, SVS 4720)
author: Ernie Wright, NASA Goddard
attributionText: Moon texture: NASA Goddard / Ernie Wright, CGI Moon Kit (SVS 4720).
alt: The Moon's near side in natural color, desktop high-DPI version, from the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Camera.

title: Artist's concept of a high-speed collision (HD 172555)
source: NASA / JPL-Caltech (Spitzer Space Telescope findings, HD 172555)
author: NASA / JPL-Caltech
attributionText: Artist's concept of a high-speed collision. NASA / JPL-Caltech (HD 172555).
alt: An artist's impression of the giant impact: two rocky worlds colliding, spraying glowing debris into space against a black background.

title: Total solar eclipse with corona and Bailey's beads, August 21, 2017 (AFRC2017-0233-005)
author: Carla Thomas, NASA
attributionText: Total solar eclipse, Aug 21, 2017. NASA / Carla Thomas (AFRC2017-0233-005).
alt: A total solar eclipse with the Sun's corona glowing around the dark silhouette of the Moon.

title: Full disc of the Moon photographed by Apollo 17 crewmen (AS17-152-23311)
source: NASA / Flickr NASA Image and Video Library
author: NASA
attributionText: Full disc of the Moon, Apollo 17, December 19, 1972. NASA (AS17-152-23311).
alt: Full disc of the Moon photographed by the Apollo 17 crew during transearth coast homeward, showing lunar maria including Serenitatis, Tranquillitatis, Nectaris, Fecunditatis, and Crisium.

title: Tycho crater central peak with summit boulder, LROC
source: NASA Goddard Photo and Video, via Flickr
author: NASA Goddard / Arizona State University, LROC
attributionText: Tycho crater central peak, LROC. NASA Goddard / Arizona State University (CC BY 2.0).
alt: An oblique view of the central peak of Tycho crater, with a large boulder perched on the summit and the terraced inner walls visible behind it.

title: Mare Orientale with sunset shadows, LRO LOLA hyperwall still (SVS 3917)
author: Ernie Wright, NASA Goddard SVS
attributionText: Mare Orientale, LRO LOLA elevation. NASA Goddard SVS / Ernie Wright (SVS 3917).
alt: An oblique view of Mare Orientale, showing its concentric rings of mountains around a darker central plain, with sunset shadows highlighting the relief.

title: Astronaut bootprint in lunar soil (AS11-40-5878)
author: Buzz Aldrin, Apollo 11
attributionText: Bootprint in the lunar soil, Apollo 11, 20 July 1969. NASA / Buzz Aldrin (AS11-40-5878).
alt: A close-up of an astronaut's bootprint pressed into the fine lunar soil during the Apollo 11 moonwalk.

title: Apollo 8 liftoff (S69-15558)
author: NASA
attributionText: Apollo 8 liftoff from Kennedy Space Center, December 21, 1968. NASA (S69-15558).
alt: Apollo 8 liftoff from Kennedy Space Center, December 21, 1968.

title: Earthrise (AS08-14-2383)
author: William Anders, Apollo 8
attributionText: Earthrise, Apollo 8, Dec 24, 1968. NASA / William Anders (AS08-14-2383).
alt: Earthrise: Earth seen above the lunar horizon, photographed by William Anders during Apollo 8 on December 24, 1968.

title: Erlanger crater at local sunrise, LROC NAC
source: Wikimedia Commons file page for NASA LROC NAC image of Erlanger crater
author: NASA / GSFC / Arizona State University
attributionText: Erlanger crater at local sunrise, LROC NAC. NASA / GSFC / Arizona State University.
alt: An overhead view of Erlanger crater near the lunar pole, with the rim lit by low sunlight while the interior stays mostly black in permanent shadow.

title: Mare Tranquillitatis pit crater, LROC NAC
source: Wikimedia Commons file page for NASA LROC NAC image of the Mare Tranquillitatis pit
author: NASA / GSFC / Arizona State University
attributionText: Mare Tranquillitatis pit, LROC NAC. NASA / GSFC / Arizona State University.
alt: A near-vertical view of the Mare Tranquillitatis pit: a dark circular opening in the mare plain, with a smooth illuminated floor and scattered boulders inside.

title: The Moon's near side and far side
author: NASA
attributionText: The Moon's near side and far side. NASA.
alt: A side-by-side comparison of the Moon's near side and far side, showing the darker maria concentrated on the near side and the brighter, more heavily cratered far side.

title: David Scott in the Command and Service Module hatch (AS09-20-3064)
author: Apollo 9 crew
attributionText: David Scott in the Command and Service Module hatch, March 1969. NASA (AS09-20-3064).
alt: David Scott in the Command and Service Module hatch, photographed from the Lunar Module.

title: Apollo 10 over the Moon (AS10-27-3873)
author: Apollo 10 crew
attributionText: The Command and Service Module photographed from the Lunar Module in lunar orbit, May 1969. NASA (AS10-27-3873).
alt: The Moon as seen from the Apollo 10 spacecraft in May 1969.

title: Buzz Aldrin on the lunar surface (AS11-40-5903)
author: Neil Armstrong, Apollo 11
attributionText: Buzz Aldrin on the Moon, July 20, 1969. NASA / Neil Armstrong (AS11-40-5903).
alt: Buzz Aldrin on the lunar surface during Apollo 11, with the reflection of Neil Armstrong visible in his visor.

title: Alan Bean on the lunar surface (AS12-49-7278)
author: Charles Conrad, Apollo 12
attributionText: Alan Bean holding a lunar soil sample container, November 1969. NASA / Charles Conrad (AS12-49-7278).
alt: Astronaut Alan Bean on the lunar surface during Apollo 12, holding a sample container.

title: Damaged Apollo 13 service module after jettison (AS13-59-8500)
author: Apollo 13 crew
attributionText: Damaged Service Module photographed from the Lunar Module, April 1970. NASA (AS13-59-8500).
alt: The damaged Apollo 13 Service Module photographed from the Lunar Module after separation, showing the blown-out panel.

title: Apollo 14 on the lunar surface (AS14-66-9306)
author: Apollo 14 crew
attributionText: The Lunar Module 'Antares' on the lunar surface, February 1971. NASA (AS14-66-9306).
alt: The Lunar Module 'Antares' on the lunar surface at Fra Mauro, February 1971.

title: Apollo 15 lunar rover at Hadley Rille (AS15-86-11603)
author: Apollo 15 crew
attributionText: James Irwin and the lunar rover at Hadley Rille, August 1971. NASA (AS15-86-11603).
alt: James Irwin and the lunar rover at Hadley Rille, with mountains in the background.

title: John Young jumping salute (AS16-113-18339)
author: Charles Duke, Apollo 16
attributionText: John Young's jumping salute, April 1972. NASA / Charles Duke (AS16-113-18339).
alt: Astronaut John Young jumping and saluting the United States flag during Apollo 16.

title: Apollo 17 on the lunar surface (AS17-140-21497)
author: Apollo 17 crew
attributionText: Harrison Schmitt standing next to a split lunar boulder, December 1972. NASA (AS17-140-21497).
alt: Harrison Schmitt standing next to a split lunar boulder at Taurus-Littrow, December 1972.

title: Earthset from Orion during Artemis II (art002e009288)
author: NASA
attributionText: Earthset, Artemis II, Apr 6, 2026. NASA (art002e009288).
alt: Earthset through the Orion spacecraft window with the blue and white crescent Earth rising above the Moon's dark surface.

title: Orion and the Eclipse (art002e016318)
author: NASA
attributionText: Solar eclipse captured from a camera mounted on the Orion spacecraft. Venus can be spotted on the left, and Saturn on the right of the Moon. NASA (art002e016318).
alt: A solar eclipse seen from Orion during Artemis II, with the Moon blocking the Sun, a glowing halo around the lunar disc, Venus visible to the left, and Saturn visible to the right.

title: Apollo 15 sample 15415, the Genesis Rock
author: NASA
attributionText: Apollo 15 sample 15415, the Genesis Rock, an anorthosite from Spur Crater. NASA (S71-42951).
alt: A pale, angular lunar anorthosite rock with a chalky white surface and a darker dust coating along one face, photographed on a black background.

title: Apollo 17 sample 70017, a high-titanium mare basalt
source: NASA Lunar Sample Curator (JSC), via Wikimedia Commons
author: NASA
attributionText: Apollo 17 sample 70017, a vesicular high-titanium mare basalt. NASA Lunar Sample Curator, JSC.
alt: A dark, rough, roughly fist-sized lunar basalt with a pitted surface and visible vesicles, photographed on a black background in the lunar sample lab.
